name: Timaliidae
description: Timaliidae, the Old World babblers, are a family of passerine birds found primarily in tropical Asia. They are active, social birds that often forage in noisy flocks through undergrowth and bamboo thickets.

children.name: Mixornis
children.description: A genus of small babblers with streaked underparts, found in forests across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Mixornis gularis
children.description: The Pin-striped Tit-babbler is a small, active babbler with yellow underparts finely streaked with black. It forages in noisy groups through the understory of forests and gardens across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Mixornis bornensis
children.description: The Bold-striped Tit-babbler is a small babbler with bold black streaks on a white throat and breast, olive upperparts, and a yellow belly. It is found in the lowland forests of Borneo, Sumatra, and the Malay Peninsula. It forages actively in the understory in small groups.

children.name: Mixornis flavicollis
children.description: The Grey-cheeked Tit-babbler has a grey cheek patch, yellow underparts with fine streaking, and olive-green upperparts. It is endemic to the island of Java in Indonesia. It inhabits forests and secondary growth, foraging in noisy groups.

children.name: Mixornis ruficeps
children.description: A tit-babbler with chestnut crown, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Mixornis maculata
children.description: A tit-babbler with heavily streaked underparts, found in the Greater Sundas and Philippines.

children.name: Timalia
children.description: A monotypic genus of babbler with a distinctive chestnut cap, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Timalia pileata
children.description: The Chestnut-capped Babbler has a bright chestnut crown, white throat, and heavily streaked breast. It inhabits grasslands, scrub, and bamboo thickets from the Himalayas to Java.

children.name: Stachyris
children.description: A genus of medium-sized babblers with stout bills, found in forests across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Stachyris nigriceps
children.description: The Grey-throated Babbler is a brownish babbler with a grey throat and breast and a black crown. It inhabits lowland and hill forests across Southeast Asia, foraging in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Stachyris chrysaea
children.description: The Golden-billed Babbler has a distinctive yellow-orange bill, olive upperparts, and yellow underparts with fine streaking. It is found in hill forests from the Himalayas through mainland Southeast Asia. It forages actively in the understory in small groups.

children.name: Stachyris leucotis
children.description: The White-necked Babbler has a distinctive white throat and neck patch, dark brown upperparts, and a pale belly. It is found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages in small groups in the understory.

children.name: Stachyris nigricollis
children.description: The Black-throated Babbler has a distinctive black throat and upper breast, chestnut crown, and olive-brown upperparts. It is found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages in noisy groups in the understory.

children.name: Stachyris maculata
children.description: The Chestnut-rumped Babbler has a distinctive chestnut rump, olive-brown upperparts, and white underparts with dark scaling on the throat. It is found in lowland forests of Borneo and Sumatra. It forages in the understory in small flocks.

children.name: Stachyris poliocephala
children.description: The Grey-headed Babbler has a grey crown and nape, olive-brown upperparts, and white underparts with a buff wash. It is found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages in small groups in the understory and mid-story.

children.name: Stachyris grammiceps
children.description: The White-breasted Babbler has a white throat and breast, a chestnut crown, and olive-brown upperparts. It is endemic to the island of Java in Indonesia. It inhabits lowland forests and forages in the understory.

children.name: Stachyris strialata
children.description: The Spot-necked Babbler has a distinctive spotted throat pattern, olive-brown upperparts, and pale underparts with a buff breast. It is found from the eastern Himalayas through mainland Southeast Asia to Sumatra. It forages in the understory in small, active groups.

children.name: Stachyris oglei
children.description: The Snowy-throated Babbler has a brilliant white throat and breast, dark brown upperparts, and a chestnut crown. It is found in the hill forests of northeastern India and adjacent Myanmar. It is considered vulnerable due to its restricted range and habitat loss.

children.name: Stachyris latistriata
children.description: A babbler with bold white streaking, found in the montane forests of the Philippines.

children.name: Stachyris demorestii
children.description: A babbler with white throat and rufous crown, found in the mountains of Borneo.

children.name: Stachyris plateni
children.description: A babbler endemic to Mindanao in the Philippines.

children.name: Stachyris whiteheadi
children.description: A babbler with chestnut face, found in the montane forests of Luzon.

children.name: Stachyris striata
children.description: A babbler with heavy streaking, endemic to Luzon in the Philippines.

children.name: Cyanoderma
children.description: A genus of small babblers with rufous crowns and olive upperparts, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Cyanoderma rufifrons
children.description: The Rufous-fronted Babbler has a bright rufous forehead and crown, olive upperparts, and pale underparts. It actively forages in the understory of broadleaf forests from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Cyanoderma erythropterum
children.description: A small babbler with chestnut wings, found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Cyanoderma chrysaeum
children.description: A striking babbler with golden-yellow underparts, found in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Cyanoderma melanothorax
children.description: A small babbler with crimson forehead, found in Sri Lanka and the Western Ghats.

children.name: Cyanoderma bicolor
children.description: A babbler from the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Cyanoderma crispirostris
children.description: A babbler from the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Macronus
children.description: A genus of babblers with distinctive loose, fluffy plumage on the back and rump, found in lowland forests of Southeast Asia. They are secretive birds that forage in the understory.

children.name: Macronus striaticeps
children.description: The Brown Tit-babbler is a small babbler with brown upperparts, a streaked throat, and pale underparts. It is found in the lowland forests and secondary growth of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages actively in small groups in the understory.

children.name: Macronus ptilosus
children.description: The Fluffy-backed Tit-babbler has distinctive loose, fluffy feathers on the back and rump, dark brown upperparts, and white underparts with dark streaking. It is found in lowland forests of Borneo and Sumatra. Its fluffy plumage gives it a distinctive appearance unlike any other babbler.

children.name: Macronus bornensis
children.description: A tit-babbler with bold black streaks on white underparts.

children.name: Dumetia
children.description: Small babblers with rufous-brown plumage, found in South Asia.

children.name: Dumetia hyperythra
children.description: A babbler with tawny underparts, found in scrub and grasslands of India and Sri Lanka.

children.name: Dumetia atrogularis
children.description: A babbler with dark grey forehead, found in Sri Lanka and India.

children.name: Pomatorhinus
children.description: Medium-sized babblers with long decurved bills, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Pomatorhinus schisticeps
children.description: A scimitar-babbler with white eyebrow, found from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pomatorhinus horsfieldii
children.description: A scimitar-babbler found in forests of the Indian subcontinent.

children.name: Pomatorhinus ferruginosus
children.description: A scimitar-babbler with bright coral-red bill, found in the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Pomatorhinus nympha
children.description: A large scimitar-babbler found in southern India.

children.name: Pomatorhinus ochraceiceps
children.description: A scimitar-babbler with red bill and ochre crown, found from the Himalayas to Vietnam.

children.name: Erythrogenys
children.description: Large scimitar-babblers with heavy curved bills and rufous cheeks.

children.name: Erythrogenys erythrogenys
children.description: A large babbler with rufous cheek patch, found from the Himalayas to Taiwan.

children.name: Erythrogenys mcclellandi
children.description: A babbler with spotted underparts, found in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Sphenocichla
children.description: A genus of babblers with distinctive wedge-shaped bills, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Sphenocichla roberti
children.description: A babbler with unusual wedge-shaped bill, found in the montane forests of Sumatra.

children.name: Sphenocichla humei
children.description: A babbler with wedge-shaped bill, found in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.
